manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: card, pass, spine, bug, school, bottle, mole, root, berry.
Your opponent's team words are: cell, police, germany, beach, spring, fighter, mail, jupiter.
Distractor words are: hospital, fall, embassy, fire, czech, box, wave.
The kill words are: belt.

message.content:
.CLUE: forest
TARGETS: root, berry, bug, mole
message.reasoning:
We need to give a single-word clue that relates to some of the team's words. Must avoid being any of the listed words (including opponent and distractor and kill). Can't include parts of them. Provide clue and list of target words separated by comma.

Our team words: card, pass, spine? Actually "spine". bug, school, bottle, mole, root, berry. We can try clue "forest" relates to root, berry, bug maybe? Also mole (animal lives forest) maybe? Also "tree" could relate to root, bark... but bark not in list. "seed"? berry, root. "nature"? many. Need to ensure clue not a listed word. "Nature" is not in list. It could relate to root, berry, bug, mole. That's four. Could also maybe "shelf"? No.

But we must be careful not to be too ambiguous causing opponent words. The clue should not be too broad to accidentally cause opponent guesses like "cell", "police", etc. "Nature" might lead to "bug" strongly, "root", "berry". Could also lead to "spine"? Not really. "School" could be "nature"? Not.

We can give clue "forest" which relates to root, berry, bug, mole. But "forest" not part of any list. Good.

But also "spine" might be tree trunk? But maybe not.

Better clue "underground" could relate to mole, root, bug maybe? But "underground" might also lead to "cell"? No. But maybe "pass"? Not.

We need to give a list of target words that we intend the partner to guess. We can include up to maybe 4. Provide clue "forest". TARGETS: root, berry, bug, mole

Check if any opponent words could be guessed mistakenly to "forest". Opponent words are cell, police, germany, beach, spring, fighter, mail, jupiter. None directly forest related. Distractors: hospital, fall, embassy, fire, czech, box, wave. None. Kill: belt.

So fine.

We must ensure clue is a single word, no other text. Output format:

CLUE: forest
TARGETS: root, berry, bug, mole

That's it
